skill.name: burp-suite-web-application-testing
skill.description: Guides authorized web application security testing with Burp Suite, including proxy setup, HTTP interception, Repeater testing, scanning, Intruder workflows, and report-focused analysis.
skill.summary: Use Burp Suite workflows to intercept, test, scan, and document authorized web application security findings.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, filesystem, and network patterns, but review shows they are Markdown examples and Burp setup references rather than executable code. The skill is dual-use and provides exploitation-oriented testing workflows, so it should publish with a security testing warning and authorization expectations.

medium_findings.title: Dual-Use Web Exploitation Workflow Guidance
medium_findings.description: The guide teaches request interception, request modification, vulnerability scanning, and proof-of-concept exploitation. It includes guardrails for authorized testing, but the same steps can be misused against systems without permission.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly frames the workflow as vulnerability discovery and exploitation while also requiring authorized targets. This supports a medium dual-use rating rather than a malicious or blocked finding.

medium_findings.title: Credential Attack and Injection Payload Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill includes Intruder attack types for credential testing and payload examples for SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, and command injection. These are non-executing examples, but they are actionable offensive testing content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples are visible in Markdown and directly describe credential testing and injection payload use. They do not execute locally, but the operational security risk is clear.

low_findings.title: Static Shell and System File Matches Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The detected shell commands, path traversal strings, and system password file references appear inside a common testing payload list. No script, hook, or automation executes these strings from the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding fenced block labels the strings as common testing payloads. There is no evidence of executable code or hidden file access.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Network Values Are Burp Setup References
low_findings.description: The localhost proxy address and Burp certificate URL are expected configuration references for Burp Suite. They do not indicate external data exfiltration or unauthorized network access by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The values are local Burp proxy and certificate setup instructions. No evidence found of calls to remote services or credential transmission.

dangerous_patterns.title: Non-Executing Injection and Traversal Payloads
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill lists SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, and command injection payload examples. They are inert Markdown content but should be used only in authorized testing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The payload list is explicit and security-relevant, but it is presented as test data rather than executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automated Intruder Credential Testing Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The Intruder section describes payload positions, attack types, and example username and password values. This is normal Burp training content but can support unauthorized credential attacks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The content clearly describes credential testing mechanics. The absence of executable automation lowers the risk below high.

content.user_title: Test Web Apps with Burp Suite
content.value_statement: Web application testing can become scattered across proxy setup, request replay, scanning, and reporting. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured Burp Suite workflow for authorized security assessments.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to configure Burp Suite proxy interception for browser traffic.
Guides request review, modification, forwarding, and HTTP history analysis.
Describes Repeater workflows for manual parameter and response testing.
Outlines Burp Professional scanning setup and result review.
Covers Intruder payload positions, attack types, and result analysis.
Provides troubleshooting steps for proxy, HTTPS, and interception issues.
limitations: Requires a separate Burp Suite installation and a configured browser.
Automated scanning guidance depends on Burp Suite Professional.
Does not verify legal authorization for any target system.
Does not replace expert validation of vulnerability findings.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Authorized Web Test
use_cases.description: Set scope, configure the browser proxy, enable interception, and capture baseline traffic before deeper testing.

use_cases.title: Investigate Suspicious Requests
use_cases.description: Use HTTP history and Repeater to compare parameter changes, response differences, and possible validation flaws.

use_cases.title: Review Scan Findings
use_cases.description: Use Burp issue details, requests, responses, and remediation notes to prepare a concise vulnerability report.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Burp Proxy
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a test browser
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure Burp Suite to intercept traffic for an authorized test application. Include proxy listener checks and HTTPS certificate setup.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze HTTP History
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic review after browsing the target
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through reviewing Burp HTTP history for an in-scope application. Focus on parameters, authentication flows, and noisy traffic filters.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Repeater Test Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Burp Repeater test plan for an authorized endpoint. Include safe parameter variations, response checks, and documentation notes.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Scan and Intruder Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me review Burp scan findings and Intruder results for an authorized assessment. Prioritize evidence, false-positive checks, and remediation guidance.

output_examples.input: I need to start intercepting a staging app with Burp.
output_examples.output: The response outlines proxy listener checks, browser proxy settings, certificate installation, and a short validation workflow.

output_examples.input: I found a parameter that changes server responses in Repeater.
output_examples.output: The response proposes controlled test variations, response comparison criteria, and notes to capture for a report.

output_examples.input: Burp Professional reported several issues after a scan.
output_examples.output: The response explains how to review issue evidence, confirm reproducibility, remove false positives, and write remediation steps.

best_practices: Confirm written authorization and scope before testing any application.
Use Burp scope filters before scanning, Intruder testing, or extensive replay.
Manually validate automated findings before including them in reports.
anti_patterns: Testing systems without explicit permission or outside the agreed scope.
Running aggressive scans or Intruder attacks without rate limits.
Reporting scanner output without confirming the evidence and impact.

faq.question: Does this skill run Burp Suite for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance for using a separate Burp Suite installation.

faq.question: Is Burp Suite Professional required?
faq.answer: No. Proxy and Repeater guidance applies to Community Edition, but automated scanning requires Professional.

faq.question: Can this help with HTTPS interception issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Burp CA certificate setup and browser proxy troubleshooting.

faq.question: Does the skill include security payload examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes common testing payloads for authorized assessments, so users must follow scope and permission rules.

faq.question: Can it create a final penetration test report?
faq.answer: It can help organize findings, evidence, and remediation notes, but expert review remains necessary.

faq.question: Is this safe for production systems?
faq.answer: Only with explicit authorization, defined scope, and rate controls. Some tests can affect application stability.
